mnemonics: U Publish Data (UPD): Universities Press (U.P.) publishes books, just like this module publishes (teaches) how to organize and access data structures efficiently.
keyPoints: This module likely covers the practical implementation and real-world applications of fundamental data structures.
The focus is on how these structures are used to solve complex computing problems efficiently.
The 'Universities Press' title suggests the content may be based on or reference a specific textbook or academic publisher's material.
commonMistakes: Focusing only on the theoretical definition of a data structure and neglecting its practical application and performance implications.
Confusing the syntax or API of one data structure (e.g., a Dictionary) with another (e.g., a List) when implementing them in code.
Assuming a data structure has a single, fixed implementation (e.g., a graph can be implemented with an adjacency list OR an adjacency matrix).
